description_long: This object is a large, curved blade resembling a machete or specialized utility knife, featuring a series of evenly spaced circular holes along the length of the blade. The blade is single-edged and has a pronounced curve, with the cutting edge on the convex side. The handle is wrapped in a textured material, likely for improved grip, and is slightly contoured for ergonomic handling. The blade appears to be made of dark metal, possibly steel, and the handle is finished in a muted, dark tone with visible wrapping. There is no visible guard between the blade and the handle, giving it a streamlined appearance. The overall design suggests it is intended for heavy-duty cutting or chopping tasks, possibly in an urban or survival context.
description: A large, curved, single-edged blade with circular holes along its length and a wrapped, contoured handle. The design is robust and utilitarian, lacking a guard and featuring a dark metallic finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved blade with circular holes and the wrapped handle.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow blade profile and the oval shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the curved blade with holes and the handle wrapping.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view emphasizing the thinness of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
attribute_handle_design: contoured, wrapped in textured fabric, dark finish
